summary: End-to-end test of the new AI Advisor feature (Claude Sonnet 4.5 via EMERGENT_LLM_KEY). 6 of 7 backend pytest tests passed; full frontend flow on /ai-advisor works (UI renders, badges Attivo + claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929, suggestion buttons send messages, real numbers from dashboard appear in replies, italian language confirmed, session persists on reload, history loaded). Single CRITICAL bug found: multi-turn conversation context is NOT retained across requests within the same session_id.

critical.issue: Claude does not remember previous messages in the same session_id. Repro: turn 1 'Ricorda il mio codice 4271. Confermami.' -> AI confirms. Turn 2 (same session_id) 'Qual era il codice?' -> AI answers 'Non mi hai fornito alcun codice cliente in precedenza. Questa è la nostra prima interazione.'. Root cause in server.py lines 561-567: a fresh LlmChat instance is built on every request and chat.send_message is invoked with only the latest user text — no rehydration of prior messages from db.chat_messages. emergentintegrations LlmChat does not auto-load history from Mongo just because session_id is reused; history lives in the in-memory instance which is discarded after each request.

action_items: FIX multi-turn context: before calling chat.send_message, replay the existing chat_messages history into LlmChat. Recommended pattern: load all prior messages for session_id from MongoDB, then for each one call something like chat.add_user_message(text)/chat.add_assistant_message(text) (or whatever the emergentintegrations API exposes — check the library) BEFORE sending the new user message. Alternative: keep an in-process dict[session_id] -> LlmChat to reuse the same instance for hot sessions, with TTL.
Optional: cap rehydrated history to e.g. last 20 turns to control token cost.
Optional: add rate-limit / message length validation (current max length is unbounded).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 561: LlmChat is rebuilt every request — kills multi-turn memory. This is the root cause of the failed test.
server.py line 547: returns HTTP 503 when EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is missing — correct.
server.py line 549-550: empty-message validation uses .strip(), HTTP 400 — correct.
server.py line 581: history fetch correctly excludes mongodb _id and sorts by created_at ascending — correct.
server.py line 568-570: any LLM exception is wrapped in HTTP 500 with raw error message — consider sanitising before exposing to client.

AIAdvisor.jsx line 28: session_id is stored in localStorage — good for persistence; but never rotated, so a user keeps the same session forever (token cost grows once multi-turn is fixed). Consider a 'New chat' button.

Portability note: emergentintegrations + EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is an Emergent-specific gateway. To run on Render/Vercel/AWS/etc., the key must remain reachable (it is — Emergent's gateway is public HTTPS), OR swap to anthropic SDK + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Both require pip install + env var; nothing else is platform-specific.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_ai_advisor.py (NEW — 7 pytest cases for the AI advisor)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (UI renders, badges, suggestions, send/reply, session persistence on reload all OK)

seed_data_creation: Used existing seeded financial data (fatturato 480000, EBITDA 206200, EBITDA% 42.96%). Test sessions session-id prefixed with 'test-ai-' and cleaned up via DELETE /api/ai-advisor/history/{session_id}.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run /app/backend/tests/test_ai_advisor.py after the fix. The single failing case is test_multi_turn_context (asks Claude to recall a code given in turn 1). All other AI behavior (status, empty validation, real-numbers EBITDA injection, italian-only response, history chronological order, DELETE clear) is verified working. Frontend on /ai-advisor is fully functional; do not re-test exhaustively unless server.py logic changes meaningfully.

rca of the issue.bug: Multi-turn AI conversation loses context across requests in the same session_id.
rca of the issue.reproduction: 1) POST /api/ai-advisor/chat {session_id: X, message: 'Ricorda 4271'}; 2) POST /api/ai-advisor/chat {session_id: X, message: 'Qual era il numero?'}; assistant replies it has no prior context.
rca of the issue.root_cause: server.py:561 instantiates a brand-new LlmChat on every request; emergentintegrations does NOT autoload history from MongoDB just because the same session_id string is passed. The chat_messages collection IS persisted (history endpoint works), but it is never replayed into LlmChat before send_message.
rca of the issue.mitigation: Before chat.send_message, fetch prior chat_messages for session_id from Mongo and replay them into the LlmChat instance using the library's add_message API; then send the new user message. Alternatively cache LlmChat instances per session in a module-level dict.
